title: leucanthemum graminifolium var. controversum
intro: Leucanthemum graminifolium var. controversum, also known as the grass-leaved white daisy, is a plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its grass-like leaves and white daisy-like flowers. Leucanthemum graminifolium var. controversum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antitussive properties. Herbal preparations of Leucanthemum graminifolium var. controversum can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
